title: DCS World Steam Edition

notes:
Failed to run after using Protontricks to install Corefonts, then also installed vcrun2017 and d3dcompiler_43, after which the game started up fine. Got in-game with good performance and settings maxed out (minus AA and then lowered depth of field for frame-rate reasons). 

Issue noticed that might or might not be proton related was rebinding controls to my HOTAS setup: DCS World after startup bound a lot of controls to various axes/buttons seemingly at random, often binding the same buttons and axes so several different in-game commands. Clearing out all control bindings and assigning them one-by-one only caused the mass binding to happen again. But individually deleting miss-assigned commands and either leaving them blank or reassigning them to a preferred control worked even after game restarts.

Had to disable steam in-game controller support to reduce conflict with what I wanted out of my HOTAS setup and what Steam thought I wanted with it.
title: DCS World Steam Edition

title: DCS World Steam Edition

notes:
So happy this works quite easily! Great performance for me! Actually better framerate than my Windows install. Runs at 60% RAM usage, Windows was around 90% with similar apps running.

This is how I got it working, I hope it works for you too! 
If it works, please give the game a *GOLD* rating, loads of the previous "Borked" ratings make it look difficult!
The button to rate should be at the bottom of the page.


I had a slight problem. Somehow I borked the DCS install first time around. Had to uninstall, and redownload 80GB. If you want to be safe, you can backup your DCS install folder after step 3.

If you have just installed Steam and no other games follow steps 1 and 2...)

1. Install Protontricks with pipx  (if possible):
https://github.com/Matoking/protontricks

2. Install winetricks to /usr/local/bin
https://github.com/Winetricks/winetricks

Previous Steamplay users:

3. Use Steam to install DCS onto Linux disk (NTFS disk causes problems)

4. Select Proton4.11-1 from Steam preferences, Steamplay page.

5. Start game once (it will fail)

6. Install extra software with Protontricks
protontricks 223750 vcrun2017 corefonts xact d3dcompiler_43
(to find protontricks you may run "locate bin/protontricks")

Start game again and it should work!
title: DCS World Steam Edition

notes:
Worked fluidly after following the instructions on this page. Seems to be completely playable. F2 (3rd person view) freezes the game for a while but doesn't crash the game.

IMPORTANT: need to use git versions of protontricks (1.2.4. https://github.com/Matoking/protontricks via pipx) and winetricks (20190615-next https://github.com/Winetricks/winetricks). The winetricks version in Ubuntu repos is too old and you'll get "this operating system is not supported" error when trying to launch DCS. Install dependencies with "protontricks 223750 vcrun2017 corefonts xact d3dcompiler_43".
title: DCS World Steam Edition

#sudo cp winetricks /usr/local/bin

##Install ProtonTricks##

#sudo apt install python3-pip python3-setuptools python3-venv
#python3 -m pip install --user pipx
#~/.local/bin/pipx ensurepath

title: DCS World Steam Edition

notes:
I tried all the suggestions (clean install, run once, then apply protontricks with all the listed commands (I tried a few variations suggested here and online), I also tried various Proton Versions (4.2.x and higher). 

I managed to get it working ONCE (the first time) but now, and even after fresh uninstalls and reinstalls, it simply fails to launch. Steam will say "Opening..." and then the button becomes "Play" again. No visible error.

I also manually updated Winetricks to the _latest_ to overcome the old debian/ubuntu version in the repos but it just doesn't work. Cannot get it to display anything.
title: DCS World Steam Edition

notes: As others described: in the first run it does not launch (but it creates the wineprefix). Then after "protontricks 223750 vcrun2017 corefonts xact d3dcompiler_43" it starts and seems to run relatively nicely (Proton 4.11-7). But: In full screen the mouse control of the camera does not work (In windowed move, it works better but not fully ok). When the game crashes and asks to collect info and send a report (it happened three times so far to me, once by pressing F10, another 2 during normal flight) the game will not start again (seems only fully uninstalling the game and installing it again helps, deleting the user saved games folder, or recovering the whole wineprefix from a backup did not help)
title: DCS World Steam Edition

notes:
As suggested below, game started fine with help of "protontricks 223750 vcrun2013 corefonts xact d3dcompiler_43" after first launch attempt.
F10 (The Map Hotkey) crashes the game. After crash I was forced to delete and create prefix to make game work again.
